---
title: "Overview and workflow"
subtitle: "The typed path from dataset to first-level fit, contrast, and group summary."
jupyter: python3
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

## Core Workflow

`fmrimod` combines timing information, event specifications, baseline terms,
first-level fitting, contrasts, and group summaries under one typed workflow:

```text
fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> contrast -> group_fit
```

A typical workflow starts with an event table, an acquisition grid, and a data
object that keeps those pieces together.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import fmrimod as fm
from fmrimod.design_colmap import design_colmap

events = pd.DataFrame({
    "onset": [0, 12, 24, 36, 48, 60],
    "stim_type": ["face", "scene", "face", "scene", "face", "scene"],
})

sf = fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=[40, 40], TR=1.0)
sf.blocklens
```

```{python}
rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
Y = rng.normal(size=(80, 6))
dataset = fm.fmri_dataset(Y, tr=1.0, events=events)
dataset.n_timepoints, dataset.n_voxels
```

## Event Model

The formula creates one HRF-convolved regressor per stimulus level. The string
form is a concise authoring surface; internally it compiles to the same typed
design-spec tree used by the object builders.

```{python}
model = fm.event_model(
    'onset ~ hrf(stim_type, basis="spmg1")',
    data=events,
    sampling_frame=fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=80, TR=1.0),
)

model.design_matrix.shape
```

```{python}
design_colmap(model)[["col", "name", "condition", "basis_name"]]
```

## Baseline and Contrasts

Baseline modeling and contrast testing are separate decisions. The design
matrix stores task regressors; contrast vectors state the scientific
comparison in that column space.

```{python}
columns = model.column_names
event_face_vs_scene = np.zeros(len(columns))
event_face_vs_scene[columns.index("stim_type_stim_type.face")] = 1
event_face_vs_scene[columns.index("stim_type_stim_type.scene")] = -1

pd.DataFrame({"column": columns, "face_vs_scene": event_face_vs_scene})
```

## First-Level Fit

Fit through the public first-level entry point. This keeps the dataset,
compiled design, baseline, solver configuration, and contrast machinery in one
inspectable result object.

```{python}
fit = fm.fmri_lm("hrf(stim_type)", dataset)
fit.betas.shape, fit.tstat().shape
```

```{python}
fit_columns = list(fit.model.design_matrix().columns)
face_vs_scene = np.zeros(len(fit_columns))
face_vs_scene[fit_columns.index("stim_type_stim_type.face")] = 1
face_vs_scene[fit_columns.index("stim_type_stim_type.scene")] = -1

contrast = fit.contrast(face_vs_scene, name="face_vs_scene")
contrast.estimate.shape, contrast.stat.shape
```

## Advanced Topics

The same design/data contract supports typed engine options, AR diagnostics,
single-trial estimation, benchmark fixtures, and native group-level inference.
Low-level matrix helpers remain useful for numerical canaries, but the
flagship path should stay on the public seam.

## Next

- [Linear model](linear-model.qmd)
- [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd)
- [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd)
